mapping.a_element: von Neumann algebra of observables available to the observer
mapping.b_element: Pauli operators/observables propagated through the quantum circuit

mapping.a_element: projective measurements recorded on the worldline
mapping.b_element: expectation values evaluated during circuit simulation

brief_justification: The only overlap is generic quantum-operator vocabulary; the actual machinery differs entirely. Paper A builds a simplicial complex from algebra projections to reconstruct the topology of a fluctuating spacetime (an algebraic-topology construction), whereas Paper B is a reverse-mode automatic-differentiation (backpropagation) scheme over Pauli propagation to compute parameter gradients for optimizing quantum circuits. There is no role-for-role correspondence between simplicial/topological reconstruction and gradient-based circuit optimization.
